title: FAF Agent (.fafa)
description: Schema for application/vnd.fafa+yaml v1.0 — the FAF Agent Card format: a declarative, vendor-neutral YAML declaration of an agent's identity, the capabilities it exposes, and the endpoints through which it is reached. Declarative, never executable. Unknown fields are forward-compatible extensions and MUST be ignored by consumers (additionalProperties: true throughout). Open vocabularies (capability type, endpoint protocol/transport) are validated as strings, not closed enums. Canonical specification: https://github.com/Wolfe-Jam/faf/blob/main/AGENT-FORMAT.md

version.description: Version of THIS specification the document conforms to (e.g. "1.0"). Distinct from the agent's own version (agent.version).

name.description: Agent name; unique within the issuing vendor namespace.

displayName.description: Human-readable display name, distinct from the machine `name`/id. Used by projections such as the A2A AgentCard (which requires a human name).

id.description: Globally unique identifier — DID, URI, or vendor-scoped UUID.

version.description: Semantic version of the AGENT, NOT of this specification.

capabilities.description: What the agent can do. MAY be empty. Declaration is not authorization (a consumer MUST NOT auto-execute a capability purely because it is declared).

name.description: Capability name; unique within the document.

type.description: Capability kind. Defined values: tool, resource, prompt, event. Other values are permitted (open vocabulary).

apophatic.description: If true, the capability refuses out-of-scope invocation rather than attempting best-effort. A FAF-family convention.

cites_spec.description: The format/spec the capability operates against, e.g. application/vnd.faf+yaml. A FAF-family convention.

endpoints.description: How to reach the agent. MUST be non-empty. Multiple endpoints MAY declare the same protocol with different transports.

protocol.description: Defined values: mcp, a2a, grpc, http. Other values are permitted (open vocabulary).

transport.description: Defined values: stdio, http, websocket, quic.

location.description: Reachable address — URI, package name, or transport-specific locator.

health.description: Optional health endpoint or indicator (format implementation-defined).

attachment.description: Optional infrastructure context within which the agent operates. Requires no particular gateway protocol.

provenance.description: Optional context substrate the agent reads from — the FAF-family integration point. An agent SHOULD declare which .faf it reads.

faf.description: Reference to a .faf document — path, URL, or namepoint.

spec.description: Media type of the substrate, e.g. application/vnd.faf+yaml.

signature.description: Optional provenance/integrity material. Where present, consumers MUST treat endpoints as untrusted until verified against it. Verification is the consumer's responsibility.
